verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease of the kallikrein family, synthesized as a prepro-enzyme, processed through the secretory pathway, and released into prostatic fluid / seminal plasma / serum. It has no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented covalent or wash-resistant tethering to a cell-surface partner. Clinical interest (e.g., radioligand programs) targets the soluble secreted enzyme, sometimes captured transiently on tumor cells, but this is reversible non-covalent recruitment — fails the wash test.
